senses.definition: a party held for a man by his male friends, who is about to get married

examples.x: After the bachelor party, the groom and his friends had great stories and memories to share as they prepared for the upcoming wedding.
The groom's friends planned a bachelor party at a beach resort, complete with games, a barbecue, and a night out on the town.
The best man took charge of organizing the bachelor party, ensuring it was a special and memorable event for the groom.
Some bachelor parties include adventurous activities like skydiving or a weekend camping trip, reflecting the groom's interests.

senses.definition: a party for a woman that is held before her marriage and is often arranged and attended by her female friends

examples.x: The bridesmaids organized a fun-filled bachelorette party with a spa day followed by a night out on the town.
They surprised the bride with a themed bachelorette party at a beach resort, complete with games and cocktails.
The bachelorette party included a cooking class where everyone learned to make gourmet dishes together.
The bride-to-be enjoyed a relaxing weekend getaway with her closest friends for her bachelorette party.
Bachelorette parties often feature personalized decorations, party favors, and memorable experiences to celebrate the bride's upcoming wedding.

senses.definition: a man chosen by a bridegroom to help him at his wedding

examples.x: In the days leading up to the wedding, the best man helps coordinate the groom’s schedule, ensuring that he is on time for all pre-wedding events.
The best man may also help with managing the groomsmen, ensuring they are prepared and coordinated for their roles in the wedding.
The best man often holds onto the wedding rings during the ceremony, ensuring they are safely delivered to the officiant at the right moment.
On the wedding day, the best man assisted the groom with getting dressed and made sure he had everything he needed for the ceremony.

senses.definition: a woman or girl chosen by a bride to help her at her wedding

examples.x: The bridesmaids assisted the bride with selecting her wedding dress and coordinating their own outfits to match the wedding theme.
On the wedding day, the bridesmaids supported the bride emotionally and physically, helping her with any last-minute preparations and adjustments.
The bridesmaids helped organize the bridal shower and bachelorette party, ensuring that the events were memorable and enjoyable for the bride.
During the ceremony, the bridesmaids walked down the aisle in pairs, carrying bouquets and standing by the bride’s side.
She felt proud to stand beside her best friend as a bridesmaid.

senses.definition: a man who is engaged to someone
a woman who is engaged to someone
a young girl who throws flower petals in front of a bride at a wedding

examples.x: The flower girl looked adorable in her white dress and floral crown as she walked down the aisle.
She practiced scattering flower petals along the wedding aisle for her role as the flower girl.
The bride's niece was chosen to be the flower girl and felt excited to play such an important role in the wedding.
The flower girl's entrance brought smiles to the faces of all the wedding guests.
Flower girls traditionally symbolize innocence and purity, adding charm and sweetness to the wedding ceremony.

senses.definition: a woman chosen by the bride to be her main attendant and support her throughout the wedding planning process and on the wedding day itself

examples.x: The maid of honor gave a heartfelt toast at the wedding reception, celebrating the bride and groom.
The maid of honor organized a memorable bachelorette party for the bride and her closest friends.
During the ceremony, the maid of honor held the bride's bouquet and assisted with her gown.
The bride and her maid of honor have been best friends since childhood, making her role even more special.

senses.definition: the flowers that are attractively arranged for a ceremony or as a gift

examples.x: The bride held a bouquet of white roses and lilies as she walked down the aisle, complementing her wedding dress.
For her birthday, she received a colorful bouquet of mixed flowers, including tulips, daisies, and roses.
The florist arranged a bouquet of seasonal flowers for the wedding, featuring peonies, hydrangeas, and ranunculus.
The groom presented his fiancée with a bouquet of her favorite flowers as a romantic gesture on their engagement day.

senses.definition: a formal party held to celebrate an event or welcome someone

examples.x: After the ceremony, the couple hosted a reception where guests enjoyed a variety of appetizers and a formal meal.
At the reception, the bride and groom cut the wedding cake, and the guests joined in for a toast to their future together.
The reception included live music and a DJ, providing entertainment and dancing opportunities for all the attendees.
The reception featured a photo booth where guests could take fun pictures to remember the celebration.
The bride and groom greeted guests at the reception.

senses.definition: a formal talk about a particular topic given to an audience

examples.x: The politician delivered an inspiring speech at the campaign rally.
She prepared a persuasive speech to convince her classmates to support the recycling initiative.
The commencement speech at graduation encouraged students to pursue their dreams.
The CEO's annual speech outlined the company's achievements and future goals.
He practiced his acceptance speech in front of the mirror before the award ceremony.

senses.definition: the act of raising a glass, usually filled with alcohol, in honor of someone or to wish them health, happiness, or success

examples.x: The best man gave a touching toast to the newlyweds at the wedding reception.
She raised her glass in a toast to celebrate her promotion with her colleagues.
The host proposed a toast to commemorate the guest of honor's achievements.
We gathered around the table and clinked glasses, offering a toast to friendship and good times.
He made a heartfelt toast to his parents on their wedding anniversary, expressing gratitude and love.

senses.definition: a ring that someone gives their partner after agreeing to marry each other

examples.x: He proposed to her with a sparkling diamond engagement ring, which she accepted with tears of joy.
The engagement ring was a classic solitaire diamond set in a simple gold band, reflecting her elegant taste.
She admired her engagement ring, which featured a halo of smaller diamonds surrounding a central gem.
The couple visited a jeweler to resize the engagement ring to ensure a perfect fit.
He chose the engagement ring with great care, considering her preferences and style.

senses.definition: a ring that someone's spouse gives them during their wedding ceremony

examples.x: She lost her wedding ring at the beach.
His wedding ring was engraved with their wedding date.
They exchanged wedding rings during the ceremony.
He never takes off his wedding ring.
The jeweler helped them choose a matching wedding ring set.

senses.definition: a formal dress worn by a bride during their wedding ceremony

examples.x: The bride walked down the aisle in a stunning wedding gown with intricate lace details and a flowing train.
She selected a classic white wedding gown with a fitted bodice and a full skirt for her traditional ceremony.
The wedding gown featured a modern design with a sleek silhouette and minimal embellishments, matching her contemporary style.
During the fitting, she tried on several wedding gowns before finding the perfect one that made her feel like a princess.
After the ceremony, the bride’s wedding gown was carefully preserved in a special box to keep it in pristine condition for future generations.

senses.definition: a piece of fabric worn by brides that covers the head and face or drapes over the back, often made of lace or other delicate materials

examples.x: The bride wore a long, flowing veil that cascaded down her back during the wedding ceremony.
Tradition dictates that the groom lifts the bride's veil before kissing her after exchanging vows.
Her grandmother's lace veil, passed down through generations, added a sentimental touch to her wedding attire.
The bride's veil fluttered in the breeze as she walked down the aisle, creating a magical and ethereal effect.

senses.definition: a formal men's suit typically worn for black-tie events and formal occasions

examples.x: The groom and his groomsmen wore classic black tuxedos with white dress shirts and black bow ties for the wedding.
At the formal gala, the attendees were required to dress in tuxedos, ensuring a sophisticated and elegant atmosphere.
He rented a tuxedo for the prom, opting for a sleek, navy-blue version with a white shirt and black patent leather shoes.
The tuxedo featured a subtle pattern on the jacket’s lapels, adding a modern twist to the traditional formalwear.
He chose a classic black tuxedo for his best friend’s wedding, completing the look with a crisp white pocket square.

senses.definition: the passageway between rows of seats in a church, often leading from the entrance to the altar

examples.x: The bride walked gracefully down the aisle, her dress trailing behind her.
The flower girl scattered petals along the aisle before the wedding ceremony began.
Guests stood and turned to watch the groom as he made his way up the aisle.
During the service, the priest walked up and down the aisle, blessing the congregation.

senses.definition: small pieces of colored paper thrown during a special event, particularly over the newlyweds after their wedding ceremony

examples.x: After the couple said their vows, guests threw confetti over them as they exited the ceremony, creating a colorful shower.
The parade featured floats adorned with confetti, adding to the vibrant and festive mood of the event.
As the clock struck midnight on New Year's Eve, confetti fell from the ceiling, marking the start of the new year with a splash of color.
The team won the championship, and fans celebrated by throwing confetti into the air, cheering and reveling in the victory.

senses.definition: to run away secretly and marry one's partner

examples.x: Despite their families' objections, the young couple decided to elope and get married.
Jane and John decided to elope and celebrate their love in a small chapel by the beach.
The young couple faced opposition from their families, leading them to elope quietly.
Mark and Maria made the spontaneous decision to elope in a charming European city.

senses.definition: to give something to someone and receive something else from them

examples.x: They decided to exchange gifts during the holiday celebration.
The students agreed to exchange study notes to prepare for the exam.
In the marketplace, people regularly exchange goods and services.
The conference provided an opportunity for professionals to exchange ideas and insights in their respective fields.

senses.definition: a serious and formal promise, made especially during a wedding or religious ceremony

examples.x: The couple exchanged vows during the wedding ceremony, pledging to love and support each other for the rest of their lives.
She made a vow to her family to always be there for them, no matter what challenges they faced.
At the graduation ceremony, the students took a vow to uphold the values and standards of their institution.
As part of the initiation ritual, the members made a vow to uphold the traditions and responsibilities of their organization.

senses.definition: a metal cup-shaped object with a separate piece of metal hanging inside that makes a ringing noise when it moves

examples.x: The bell on the restaurant's counter jingled when customers entered or exited the establishment.
During the event, a large bell was rung to mark the beginning of each new activity.
During the festival, a large brass bell was used to attract people’s attention to the opening ceremony.
She adjusted the tiny bell on her cat’s collar to make sure she could hear when the cat was nearby.

senses.definition: a specific area at an event, a disco, club, etc. where people can dance

examples.x: The newlyweds shared their first dance on the beautifully decorated dance floor.
The DJ played upbeat music that filled the dance floor with energetic guests.
The dance floor was illuminated with colorful lights, creating a festive atmosphere.
Friends and family gathered on the dance floor to celebrate the couple's anniversary.
She enjoyed dancing with her friends on the spacious dance floor at the nightclub.

senses.definition: a holiday taken by newlyweds immediately after their wedding

examples.x: They planned a luxurious honeymoon to an exotic island resort in the Caribbean.
The couple enjoyed exploring historic landmarks during their European honeymoon.
Their honeymoon destination was a secluded beachfront villa overlooking the turquoise waters of the Indian Ocean.
They spent their honeymoon hiking in the mountains and enjoying the scenic beauty of nature.
The honeymoon was a time for them to unwind, create lasting memories, and embark on new adventures together.

senses.definition: someone who has recently gotten married

examples.x: The newlyweds spent their first week of marriage on a romantic honeymoon in Paris.
At the reception, the newlyweds shared their first dance as a married couple, surrounded by their loved ones.
The newlywed's home was filled with wedding photos and mementos from their recent ceremony.
As a surprise, their friends threw a party to welcome the newlyweds back from their honeymoon.
Everyone admired the newlyweds during the reception.

senses.definition: (of a woman or a female animal) carrying a baby inside one's body

examples.x: The pregnant cat nested in a quiet corner of the house as she prepared to give birth to her litter of kittens.
Sarah's pregnant sister was glowing with joy as she shared the news of her upcoming addition to the family.
The pregnant elephant had a gestation period of nearly two years before giving birth to her calf.
David's pregnant wife experienced cravings and mood swings as her body adjusted to the changes of pregnancy.
Despite being pregnant with twins, Mary continued to work and maintain her daily routine.
